title: JRN Multiplicative Compounding: 2×2 Probe Grid on rel-f1 with Alternative Compounding Models
summary: Demo notebook measuring Join Reproduction Number (JRN) across 13 foreign-key joins in the RelBench rel-f1 (Formula 1) relational database. It computes individual, enrichment, and chain JRN using MLP and GBM probes, then compares four compounding models (multiplicative, additive, bottleneck, log-linear) to predict multi-hop chain JRN from single-hop values. The mini data file contains a curated subset of the rel-f1 schema (9 tables, 6 tasks) with sample rows and metadata.
